info.name: MediaFlow API
info.description:
## Overview

Asynchronous media conversion API built with **FastAPI**, **Celery**, **FFmpeg**, and **MinIO**. Upload images or videos, receive a `job_id`, and poll for the result.

---

## Quick Start

1. Set `email` and `password` in the **Variables** tab.
2. Run **Register** or **Login** — `accessToken` and `refreshToken` are saved automatically.
3. Upload a file to any **Image** or **Video** endpoint — `jobId` is saved automatically.
4. Run **Get Job Status** until `status == "completed"`.
5. The presigned download URL is stored in `resultUrl`.

---

## Response Envelope

All responses follow a standard envelope (except `POST /auth/login` and `204 No Content`):

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { ... },
  "message": "optional message"
}
```

---

## Authentication

All endpoints except **Health Check**, **Register**, and **Login** require a `Bearer` token. The collection-level auth is pre-configured to use `{{accessToken}}`.

---

## Rate Limits

| Scope | Limit |
|---|---|
| `POST /image/*` | 30 req / min |
| `POST /video/convert`, `/rotate`, `/resize`, `/trim` | 10 req / min |
| `POST /video/thumbnail` | 20 req / min |

---

## Job Lifecycle

`pending` → `started` → `completed` | `failed` | `cancelled`

exec: // Global: assert JSON content-type on all non-204 responses.

variable.description: API base URL. Change to match your deployment (e.g. https://api.yourapp.com).
Email address used for registration and login.
Password for registration and login. Use Postman Vault to store secrets in team environments.
JWT access token (TTL: 30 min by default). Auto-populated by Register and Login.
JWT refresh token (TTL: 7 days by default). Auto-populated by Register and Login.
Authenticated user UUID. Auto-populated by Register.
Last queued conversion job UUID. Auto-populated by any Image or Video endpoint.
Presigned MinIO download URL. Auto-populated by Get Job Status when the job is completed.

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description:
User registration, login, token refresh, and current-user profile.

Running **Register** or **Login** automatically saves `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, and `userId` to collection variables.

request.description:
Create a new user account.

On success (`201 Created`), the response contains `data.tokens` and `data.user`. The test script automatically saves `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, and `userId` to collection variables.

**Request Body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Constraints |
|---|---|---|---|
| `email` | string | ✓ | Valid email format |
| `password` | string | ✓ | Min 1 character |

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "user": { "id": "...", "email": "...", "is_active": true, "created_at": "..." },
    "tokens": { "access_token": "...", "refresh_token": "...", "token_type": "bearer" }
  },
  "message": "Registration successful"
}
```

exec: console.log('accessToken, refreshToken, and userId saved from registration.');

urlencoded.description: User email address. The OAuth2 spec refers to this field as 'username'.

request.description:
Authenticate and obtain a token pair (OAuth2 Password Flow).

> ⚠️ This endpoint uses `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` (not JSON) and returns a **flat** OAuth2 token response instead of the standard envelope.

**Request Body (form-encoded):**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `username` | string | ✓ | User email (OAuth2 spec uses this field name) |
| `password` | string | ✓ | User password |

**Response:**
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "token_type": "bearer"
}
```

Both tokens are saved automatically by the test script.

exec: console.log('accessToken and refreshToken saved from login.');

request.description:
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair. Use this when the access token expires (default TTL: 30 min).

The new tokens are saved automatically by the test script.

**Request Body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `refresh_token` | string | ✓ | A valid, non-expired refresh token |

exec: console.warn('refreshToken is empty — run Login or Register first.');

item.name: 🏥 Health
item.description: System health check. Verifies all service dependencies are reachable. No authentication required.

request.description:
Checks all system dependencies in parallel and returns their individual status.

Always returns `200 OK` — inspect individual component statuses in the response body to detect degraded services.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "database": "ok",
    "redis": "ok",
    "minio": "ok",
    "ffmpeg": "ok",
    "cwebp": "ok"
  },
  "message": "All systems operational"
}
```

item.name: 🖼️ Image Processing
item.description:
Asynchronously convert and resize images using `cwebp` and FFmpeg.

**Supported input formats:** JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, TIFF, BMP

All endpoints:
- Accept `multipart/form-data`
- Validate MIME type via `libmagic` (not file extension)
- Return `202 Accepted` with `{ job_id, status: "pending" }`
- Save `jobId` automatically via test script

**Rate limit:** 30 requests / minute

formdata.description: Image to convert. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, TIFF, BMP.
WebP compression quality (0–100). Lower = smaller file. Default: 80.
Enable lossless WebP compression (true/false). When true, quality is ignored. Default: false.

request.description:
Convert an image to **WebP** format using `cwebp`. The conversion runs in a background Celery worker.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Image file to convert |
| `quality` | int | 80 | Compression quality (0–100) |
| `lossless` | bool | false | Lossless compression mode |

**Response (`202 Accepted`):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { "job_id": "<uuid>", "status": "pending" },
  "message": "Job queued"
}
```

The `jobId` collection variable is auto-saved. Poll **Get Job Status** for the result.

formdata.description: Image to convert. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, TIFF, BMP.
AVIF quality level (0–63). 0 = lossless, 63 = lowest quality. Default: 60.

request.description:
Convert an image to **AVIF** format using FFmpeg with the `libaom-av1` encoder.

AVIF offers significantly better compression than JPEG and WebP at equivalent visual quality, but encoding is CPU-intensive.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Image file to convert |
| `quality` | int | 60 | AV1 quality (0 = lossless, 63 = worst) |

**Response (`202 Accepted`):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { "job_id": "<uuid>", "status": "pending" },
  "message": "Job queued"
}
```

formdata.description: Image to resize. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, TIFF, BMP.
Target width in pixels. Omit to scale proportionally based on height alone.
Target height in pixels. Omit to scale proportionally based on width alone.
Resize strategy: 'cover' (crop to fill, no letterbox), 'contain' (letterbox), 'fill' (stretch to exact dimensions). Default: cover.

request.description:
Resize an image to the specified dimensions using FFmpeg.

At least one of `width` or `height` must be provided.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Image to resize |
| `width` | int or null | null | Target width in pixels |
| `height` | int or null | null | Target height in pixels |
| `fit` | string | `cover` | Resize mode: `cover`, `contain`, or `fill` |

**Fit modes:**
- `cover` — Crop to exactly fill the target rectangle (no empty space).
- `contain` — Scale to fit within the target rectangle, adding letterboxing.
- `fill` — Stretch to exact dimensions, ignoring aspect ratio.

formdata.description: Image to convert. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, TIFF, BMP.
Target format: png, jpg, jpeg, gif, bmp, tiff, tif, webp, avif.
Quality hint (0–100). Used for JPEG output. Default: 85.

request.description:
Convert an image to **any supported raster format** using `ffmpeg`.

Supported output formats: `png`, `jpg`/`jpeg`, `gif`, `bmp`, `tiff`/`tif`, `webp`, `avif`.

**Response (`202 Accepted`):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { "job_id": "<uuid>", "status": "pending" },
  "message": "Job queued"
}
```

formdata.description: First image file. Add more 'files' fields for additional images (max 20).
WebP quality (0–100). Default: 80.
Lossless WebP (true/false). Default: false.
Image file(s) to convert (max 20). Add multiple 'files' fields.
AVIF quality/CRF (0–63). Lower = better. Default: 60.
Image file(s) to convert (max 20). Add multiple 'files' fields.
Target format: png, jpg, jpeg, gif, bmp, tiff, tif, webp, avif.
Quality hint (0–100). Default: 85.

item.name: 🎬 Video Processing
item.description:
Asynchronously process video files using FFmpeg.

**Supported input formats:** MP4, WebM, MKV (Matroska), MOV (QuickTime), AVI, MPEG, OGG, 3GPP

All endpoints:
- Accept `multipart/form-data`
- Validate MIME type via `libmagic`
- Return `202 Accepted` with `{ job_id, status: "pending" }`
- Save `jobId` automatically via test script
- Use `os.cpu_count()` threads for FFmpeg automatically

**Rate limits:**
- Convert / Rotate / Resize / Trim: 10 req / min
- Thumbnail: 20 req / min

formdata.description: Video to transcode. Accepted: MP4, WebM, MKV, MOV, AVI, MPEG, OGG, 3GPP.
Output container format: mp4, webm, mkv, mov, avi. Default: mp4.
Video codec: libx264, libx265, libvpx-vp9, libaom-av1. Default: libx264.
Constant Rate Factor — quality control (0 = lossless, 51 = worst). Default: 23.
Encoding speed preset. Slower presets produce smaller files at the cost of CPU time. Options: ultrafast, superfast, veryfast, faster, fast, medium, slow, slower, veryslow. Default: medium.

request.description:
Transcode a video to a different format and/or codec using FFmpeg.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Video to transcode |
| `output_format` | string | `mp4` | Output container format |
| `codec` | string | `libx264` | Video codec |
| `crf` | int | 23 | Constant Rate Factor (quality) |
| `preset` | string | `medium` | Encoding speed vs. compression tradeoff |

**Common codec/format pairings:**
- H.264 → MP4: `codec=libx264`, `output_format=mp4`
- H.265 → MP4: `codec=libx265`, `output_format=mp4`
- VP9 → WebM: `codec=libvpx-vp9`, `output_format=webm`
- AV1 → MKV: `codec=libaom-av1`, `output_format=mkv`

formdata.description: Video to rotate. Accepted: MP4, WebM, MKV, MOV, AVI, MPEG, OGG, 3GPP.
Rotation angle. Must be exactly 90, 180, or 270. Values outside this set return 422. Default: 90.
If true, rotation is written to the container metadata only (stream copy, no re-encoding). Faster, but not supported by all players. Default: false.

request.description:
Rotate a video by 90°, 180°, or 270° clockwise.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Video to rotate |
| `degrees` | int | 90 | Rotation angle — must be **90**, **180**, or **270** |
| `no_transcode` | bool | false | Metadata-only rotation (stream copy, no re-encode) |

> ⚠️ Sending any value other than 90, 180, or 270 returns `422 Unprocessable Entity`.

formdata.description: Video to scale. Accepted: MP4, WebM, MKV, MOV, AVI, MPEG, OGG, 3GPP.
Target width in pixels. Omit to scale proportionally from height. At least one dimension is required.
Target height in pixels. Omit to scale proportionally from width. At least one dimension is required.
Preserve aspect ratio (true/false). If false, the video is stretched to the exact target dimensions. Default: true.

request.description:
Scale a video to the specified dimensions using FFmpeg.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Video to scale |
| `width` | int or null | null | Target width in pixels |
| `height` | int or null | null | Target height in pixels |
| `keep_aspect` | bool | true | Preserve original aspect ratio |

formdata.description: Video to trim. Accepted: MP4, WebM, MKV, MOV, AVI, MPEG, OGG, 3GPP.
Clip start time. Accepts HH:MM:SS format or decimal seconds (e.g. '10' or '1.5'). Required.
Clip end time. Accepts HH:MM:SS format or decimal seconds. Must be after start_time. Required.

request.description:
Extract a clip from a video between two timestamps using a fast **stream copy** (no re-encoding, near-instant for large files).

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | ✓ | Source video |
| `start_time` | string | ✓ | Clip start — `HH:MM:SS` or decimal seconds |
| `end_time` | string | ✓ | Clip end — `HH:MM:SS` or decimal seconds |

> ℹ️ Stream copy means the output container timestamps may have a small offset relative to the requested times, aligned to the nearest keyframe.

formdata.description: Source video. Accepted: MP4, WebM, MKV, MOV, AVI, MPEG, OGG, 3GPP.
Timestamp at which to capture the frame. Accepts HH:MM:SS or decimal seconds. Default: 00:00:01.

request.description:
Extract a single frame from a video at the specified timestamp and encode it as a **WebP** image.

**Parameters:**

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | File | — | Source video |
| `timestamp` | string | `00:00:01` | Frame capture timestamp (`HH:MM:SS` or decimal seconds) |

The output is a lossless WebP image stored in MinIO. Download it via the presigned `result_url` once the job is completed.

item.name: 📋 Jobs
item.description:
Track, retrieve, and cancel conversion jobs.

### Polling Workflow

1. Submit a file to any Image or Video endpoint → `jobId` is saved.
2. Repeat **Get Job Status** until `status == "completed"`.
3. `resultUrl` is populated automatically — use it to download your file.

Presigned URLs expire after `PRESIGNED_URL_EXPIRE` seconds (default: 3600).

### Job Status Values

| Status | Description |
|---|---|
| `pending` | Queued, waiting for a worker |
| `started` | Worker has picked up the job |
| `completed` | Output file is ready in MinIO |
| `failed` | Processing failed (see `error` field) |
| `cancelled` | Job was cancelled by the user |

### Job Type Values

`image_convert_webp` · `image_convert_avif` · `image_resize` · `video_convert` · `video_rotate` · `video_resize` · `video_trim` · `video_thumbnail`

query.description: Filter by status: pending | started | completed | failed | cancelled.
Filter by type: image_convert_webp | image_convert_avif | image_resize | video_convert | video_rotate | video_resize | video_trim | video_thumbnail.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all conversion jobs belonging to the authenticated user, ordered from newest to oldest.

**Query Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `page` | int | 1 | Page number |
| `page_size` | int | 20 | Items per page (max: 100) |
| `status` | string | — | Optional status filter |
| `job_type` | string | — | Optional type filter |

Enable the disabled query params above to filter results.

Retrieve the full details and current status of a specific job.

When `status == "completed"`, `data.result_url` contains a **presigned MinIO download URL** valid for `PRESIGNED_URL_EXPIRE` seconds (default: 1 hour). The URL is automatically saved to the `resultUrl` collection variable.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "<uuid>",
    "status": "completed",
    "job_type": "image_convert_webp",
    "result_url": "https://minio.host/bucket/output/...",
    "error": null,
    "created_at": "...",
    "updated_at": "..."
  }
}
```

> ℹ️ Poll this endpoint every 2–5 seconds until `status` is `completed` or `failed`.

exec: console.warn('jobId is empty — run an Image or Video endpoint first.');
console.log('resultUrl saved. Your file is ready to download.');
console.log('Job is still processing. Poll again in a few seconds.');

request.description:
Cancel and permanently delete a job.

- If the job is `pending` or `started`, the Celery task is revoked.
- Input and output files are removed from MinIO.
- Returns `204 No Content` on success.
- The `jobId` and `resultUrl` collection variables are cleared automatically by the test script.

> ⚠️ This action is irreversible. Both the job record and any associated files are permanently deleted.

exec: console.warn('jobId is empty — nothing to cancel.');
console.log('Job deleted. jobId and resultUrl cleared.');
